title: Intermediate single-page application

canonicalExplanation.summary: A React SPA project integrates semantic components, state ownership, data states, routes, errors, tests, build, and deployment fallback.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A portfolio app supports direct route refresh, loading and error recovery, keyboard navigation, tests, and a verified production bundle. Your goal is to deliver and explain a reproducible React SPA whose key user journeys pass automated and manual keyboard tests. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Accessibility, errors and performance; Routing and application context; Routing and integration tests; Error boundaries and recovery; Measure before optimization. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: A React SPA project integrates semantic components, state ownership, data states, routes, errors, tests, build, and deployment fallback. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: A polished success path can hide hydration, direct-route, offline, stale, mobile, accessibility, and privacy failures. This draft therefore rejects: Finishing all visible screens is sufficient evidence that an SPA is complete.

includes: Integrate routed views, forms, remote data, complete async states, accessibility, errors and tests.
Deliver and explain a reproducible React SPA whose key user journeys pass automated and manual keyboard tests.
excludes: A polished success path can hide hydration, direct-route, offline, stale, mobile, accessibility, and privacy failures.

outcomes: Deliver and explain a reproducible React SPA whose key user journeys pass automated and manual keyboard tests.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use a concrete React: Component-Based Browser Applications artifact to explain why this belief fails: Finishing all visible screens is sufficient evidence that an SPA is complete.

analogy.example: A state-driven component workshop can model Intermediate single-page application, where inputs and state produce a UI description.

limitations: A workshop does not reproduce reconciliation identity, hook ordering, concurrent rendering, effects, or browser accessibility APIs.

recap.summary: You should now be able to deliver and explain a reproducible React SPA whose key user journeys pass automated and manual keyboard tests. Keep this boundary in view: A polished success path can hide hydration, direct-route, offline, stale, mobile, accessibility, and privacy failures.
